sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Angela, presents with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with a reported 7th or 8th episode, which she believes she has passed, with symptoms resolving over the past 3 days.
She reports no current pain or blood in her urine.
She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes, with a current blood pressure reading of 150/90 and a past blood pressure reading of 180/90 while on 2.5mg of norvasc daily.
She has been taking metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
She reports a diet high in fast food and processed foods, which she is struggling to change.
She also reports occasional insomnia, which she has been managing with meditation and a sleep app.
She denies any current chest pain, belly pain, or other symptoms.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, temperature not mentioned.
Physical exam: Lungs clear bilaterally, grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, no tenderness to palpation of the abdomen or CVA.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, likely passed, and hypertension, well-controlled on norvasc 2.5mg daily; diabetes, well-controlled on metformin 500mg daily; and insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed metformin 500mg daily for diabetes, and referred the patient to a urologist to evaluate for recurrent kidney stones.
I also gave the patient a consult to nutrition to work on diet modification, aiming for a daily sodium intake of 2300mg or less.
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia, and encouraged the patient to practice proper sleep hygiene.
I advised the patient to take blood sugars before every meal to monitor her blood sugar levels.
Follow-up:
The patient is to return in 3 months for follow-up.
